pubmed_links: [Bhatt et al. (2017) Transcriptomic analysis shows decreased cortical expression of NR4A1, NR4A2 and RXRB in schizophrenia](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/27992436/); [Novak et al. (2010) Association of the orphan nuclear receptor NR4A1 with tardive dyskinesia](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/20010315/); [Bhatt et al. (2018) Nuclear receptors and neuroinflammation in schizophrenia](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/29888229/)
summary: NR4A1 (Nur77/NGFI-B) is an orphan nuclear receptor expressed in dopaminergic neurons whose cortical mRNA expression is significantly downregulated in schizophrenia; genetic variants in NR4A1 are associated with tardive dyskinesia risk in antipsychotic-treated patients. (emerging evidence)
